Adult Medicine

  • Immunizations and health screenings
  • Treatment of acute and chronic conditions
  • Family planning and birth control
  • STD testing, treatment, and protection
  • Sick visits
Your primary care provider leads a team-based approach that includes physicians, nurses, social workers, and health educators. Your health team is committed to providing you the care you need in the language you speak, with the information you need to make important decisions about your health. From regular screenings and sick visits to vaccinations and family planning, our approach puts you at the center of your care, giving you everything you need to live your healthiest life.

For Stockton Residents

Mobile Community Response: In partnership with the City of Stockton, Community Medical Centers offers an alternative to 911 for Stockton citizens in need of behavioral health services. If you or someone you know is experiencing mental distress, please call (833) 311-2273, 7 days a week, 8:00 am to 11:00 pm. This helpline is for non-violent individuals with mild to moderate symptoms.
